selectionStrategy: 15 NEW picks: 8 long-form + 7 Shorts. Mix of likely-graphic-dense (numbered titles, comparisons, leaks, infrastructure) and likely-restraint (essays, opinions). Shorts span all view-count tiers to surface format-specific patterns missed in Wave-6.

longForm.title: I Analyzed 512,000 Lines of Leaked Code. It Shows What's Coming for Your AI Tools.
longForm.why: Big-number title (512,000) — strong signal of count-up / BigNumber animation. 'Leaked code' likely surfaces terminal/code-diff inserts vote1 said Nate doesn't use — calibration test.

longForm.title: Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, Microsoft, Meta. All Building The Same Thing.
longForm.why: 5-brand listicle — direct retest of TalkingHeadOnlyNoOverlays vs NumberedListReveal hypothesis from woGB2 finding (F1). If logos appear, we have a new LogoGridReveal pattern.

longForm.title: 271 Vulnerabilities: What Mozilla's AI Found Changes Everything
longForm.why: Number-led security topic. Likely triggers BigNumberHorizontalBars (M3) — chance to deepen MED confidence.

longForm.title: Karpathy's Wiki vs. Open Brain. One Fails When You Need It Most.
longForm.why: Long deep-dive (41 min), explicit 'vs' framing — strong BeforeAfterTextComparison (H2) deepening + length suggests multiple title cards.

longForm.title: I Built a Deck With AI, Then Made a Second AI Attack It.
longForm.why: Builder / demo video — likely screen-recording inserts + adversarial framing. Novel territory: chance to spot DemoScreenRecording or AdversarialSplitScreen patterns.

longForm.title: LLM Agents: The Security Breach Pattern Nobody's Talking About
longForm.why: Pattern-naming title — Nate often illustrates 'patterns' with diagram cards. Test of PipelineFlow / TreeOfChildCards (H7/M2).

longForm.title: The Real Problem With AI Agents Nobody's Talking About
longForm.why: 37-min essay — calibration of restraint-cohort hypothesis. May confirm F1 finding (talking-head only for opinion content).

longForm.title: Claude's AI Town Voted Yes On Everything. That's Not A Good Sign.
longForm.why: Shorter long-form (11 min) about a specific experiment — likely has demo/screenshot inserts of AI Town, unique vs prior corpus.

shorts.title: OpenAI Just Gave Agents the Ability to Do Everything
shorts.why: Top-viewed Short not yet analyzed (18k) — capability-claim hook. Will test karaoke + emphasis-pill density.

shorts.title: While Execs Panic, This Skill Gets Rare
shorts.why: Career-essay Short — different content vertical than tech-tutorial Shorts already analyzed. Tests cross-vertical visual consistency.

shorts.title: Professors Notice Something's Drastically Wrong
shorts.why: Highest-view Short overall in Nate's recent feed (39k). Education vertical — must analyze for outlier patterns.

shorts.title: GPT-5.5 vs 1000 Piece Lego Set
shorts.why: Physical-comparison Short (vs Lego). Likely B-roll insert pattern — would be NOVEL vs Wave-6 corpus which was all talking-head Shorts.

shorts.title: I Built 2 AI Agents. One Had This. Total Game Changer
shorts.why: Builder Short — strong chance of UI screen-capture insert pattern.

shorts.title: How ChatGPT Became Teenagers' Best Friend
shorts.why: Low-view Short (2.1k) — possibly older / different template era. Variance check on KaraokeWordCaptions consistency.

shorts.title: Why Gemini 3.1 Pro Broke Every Benchmark
shorts.why: Benchmark Short — likely chart/percent insert in 9:16. Tests whether BenchmarkBars exists in Shorts lane.
